tickets.title: Register with verified university email
tickets.description:
As a Student, I want to create an account using my verified university email address, so that I can securely access the campus marketplace.

Acceptance Criteria:
- When a user enters a valid .edu email address, the system sends a verification request.
- When the user completes email verification, the account is activated.
- When a user enters a non-.edu email address or does not complete verification, account creation is denied.

tickets.title: Create item listing for sale
tickets.description:
As a Student, I want to create a listing for an item I no longer need, so that another student can reuse or purchase it.

Acceptance Criteria:
- A verified student can enter an item title, description, category, condition, and price.
- When all required listing information is provided, the item can be published to the marketplace.
- When required information is missing, the system prevents publishing and identifies the missing fields.

tickets.title: Scan textbook barcode for listing
tickets.description:
As a Student, I want to scan a textbook barcode with my phone camera, so that I can create a textbook listing without manually typing its ISBN and title.

Acceptance Criteria:
- When camera permission is granted, the student can scan a readable textbook barcode.
- When a recognized barcode is scanned, the system fills in the ISBN and available title information.
- When the barcode cannot be recognized, the system displays an error and allows manual entry.

tickets.title: Require safe exchange location for meetup
tickets.description:
As a Student, I want to select an approved Safe Exchange Zone for an in-person transaction, so that I can meet another student in a safer campus location.

Acceptance Criteria:
- When a transaction requires an in-person meetup, the system displays a list of university-approved Safe Exchange Zones.
- A student must select one Safe Exchange Zone before arranging the physical meetup through chat.
- The selected Safe Exchange Zone is visible to both participating students in the transaction conversa

tickets.title: Manage approved safe exchange zones
tickets.description:
As an Administrator/Staff member, I want to manage the list of approved Safe Exchange Zones, so that students are directed to appropriate campus locations for physical exchanges.

Acceptance Criteria:
- An authorized Administrator/Staff user can add a Safe Exchange Zone with a name and campus location.
- An authorized Administrator/Staff user can deactivate a zone that is no longer available.
- Students can select only zones currently marked as active.

analysis.ambiguity1: The requirement "only people with a valid '.edu' email address can create an account" is ambiguous because it does not define how the email address will be validated. It is unclear whether the system only checks the email format or requires an email verification link, and whether alumni or staff with university email addresses are allowed to register.
analysis.ambiguity2: The requirement "the app should force users to select a safe zone before they can chat" is ambiguous because it does not specify whether this applies to every conversation or only to transactions involving physical meetups. It is also unclear whether users selling digital items or only asking questions must select a meetup location before messaging.
analysis.conflictSolution: There is a conflict between the University Legal Team, which states that the application cannot process payments or handle money, and the Student Union President, who wants to collect a 2% transaction fee to fund club activities. The solution is that CampusLoop will not process payments or automatically deduct transaction fees. Students will arrange payments independently through cash or an external payment service. The app may optionally display a voluntary donation link for the Student Union, clearly separated from marketplace transactions.
analysis.nfr1: Security and Access Control: The system must allow account creation only for verified university users through a valid .edu email verification process, preventing unauthorized external users from accessing the marketplace.
analysis.nfr2: Safety: For listings that require an in-person exchange, the system must require users to select a university-approved Safe Exchange Zone before arranging a meetup.
analysis.nfr3: Legal and Financial Compliance: The system must not store payment information, process payments, or automatically collect transaction fees; users must complete payment independently outside the application.
